title: A level pool, not a churning sea
subtitle: A five-minute visualisation for installing the feeling of steady money flow after the feast-or-famine swing.
excerpt: A five-minute visualisation for the feast-or-famine cycle. Rather than working against the fear of the cycle, this practice installs an image of what steady flow feels like — a level pool rather than a churning sea. Use in the gap period, when the last flush has faded and the next payment hasn't arrived.
sourceNotes: Water-as-money imagery is a public-domain visualisation lineage, appearing across modern money-mindset work and older folk-prosperity traditions. The level-pool versus churning-sea contrast is adapted from Day 4 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025), "End the feast/famine cycle."

practice.whenToUse: Use in the gap period after a flush, when the next payment hasn't arrived and the fear is building. Also useful in the flush period if the bracing has started before the money has even left.

content.text: A five-minute visualisation for the feast-or-famine cycle. Rather than working directly against the fear of the swing, this practice installs a counter-image: what steady money flow feels like in the body. A level pool rather than a churning sea. Use it in the gap period, when the last flush has faded and the next payment hasn't arrived.

Sit somewhere quiet, feet flat on the floor. Close your eyes or let them rest soft. Three slow breaths: in for a count of four, out for a count of six. Let the out-breath be longer than the in-breath.

Picture a sea. Not a gentle one. A grey-green sea, choppy, unpredictable — a large wave arriving, then a trough, then another wave. You are trying to stand in it. The level keeps changing. You can't find the footing.
This is the feast-or-famine pattern. Notice how it feels in the body to stand in that sea. The tension, the bracing, the watching for the next wave.

Now let the sea settle. In your mind, watch it flatten and still. It becomes a pool — deep, wide, the water clear and level. You are standing at the edge, not in the middle of the chop. The surface is calm. Water comes in steadily from one side; water flows out gently on the other. The level does not change.
Notice what the body does as the image settles. The jaw, the shoulders, the chest. Let the image hold for two slow minutes. The pool is steady. The pool stays level.

Let the image fade. Stay with eyes closed for thirty more seconds. Then open the eyes slowly. If any detail of the pool stayed with you, a colour, a temperature, a feeling about the level, write one line. That detail is the part of the practice that has landed.
If the fear is too loud for the image to settle, tap first.
addresses the cycle directly before the visualisation. The pool image often lands more easily after a tapping round.

Water-as-money imagery is a public-domain visualisation lineage with roots in folk-prosperity traditions and modern money-mindset work. The level-pool versus churning-sea contrast is adapted from Day 4 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025), which addresses the feast-or-famine cycle and the nervous system's response to unpredictable income.
